sections.paragraph:
Ἡρῴδης, ου, ὁ [ἥρως ‘hero’, -ειδης (suffix signifying similarity)] Herod, name of Idumean rulers in Palestine

—1. Herod I, the Great (born c. 73 bc; c. 41 bc appointed by the Roman Senate; reigned 37–4 bc) Mt 2:1-22; Lk 1:5; Ac 23:35.

—2. Herod Antipas, son of Herod I (born c. 20 bc; tetrarch [called king] 4 bc–39 ad) Mt 14:1-6; Mk 6:14-22; 8:15; Lk 3:1, 19; 8:3; 9:7-9; 13:31; 23:7-15; Ac 4:27; 13:1.

—3. Herod Agrippa I (born 10 bc; reigned 37–44 ad) = M. Julius Agrippa, grandson of Herod I, called Herod in Ac 12:1 and freq. in the ch.
